mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and recession onset, triggering rapid institutional de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of monthly observations where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and housing wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields a strategy Sharpe ratio below 0.15 over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and margin compression, forcing immediate equity repricing and negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60 percent of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with stable or falling federal funds rates indicates passive central bank policy amid deteriorating labor conditions, accelerating bear market momentum and negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows zero statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals immediate economic stagnation, prompting algorithmic de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a correlation coefficient below 0.05 with 60-day forward returns over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation exceeding 3.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0 percent creates negative real rates that erode purchasing power and force eventual aggressive tightening, depressing 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The negative real rate regime produces positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 65 percent of monthly observations, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for term premium.
